name: Aider
description: Open-source CLI pair-programmer with strong git integration

developer.comment: Created by Paul Gauthier; maintained as a community open-source project under the Aider-AI GitHub org.

first-release.comment: GitHub repository created 2023-05-09; used as the first-public-release proxy.

status.comment: Actively developed; last repo push 2026-05-22, ~45.7k GitHub stars as of check date 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Apache License 2.0 (OSI-approved); confirmed via GitHub repo metadata 2026-06-02.

specialization.comment: Broad coding pair-programmer; not positioned for a narrower niche.

interface.comment: Primarily a terminal/CLI tool; also offers an optional browser-based web chat. Watch mode lets it work alongside any editor but it is not itself an IDE extension.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal and a browser web chat. Watch mode monitors files edited in any editor (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim) via AI comments rather than installing as a host-IDE extension.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Installed and run as a standalone CLI tool (pip/pipx/uv/Docker).

default-model-provider.comment: No single bundled default; works best with top models across providers (Gemini 2.5 Pro, Claude Sonnet, DeepSeek, OpenAI). User picks the model/provider.

model-choice.comment: Supports 15+ providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, Groq, Azure, Ollama, OpenRouter, etc.).

bring-your-own-key.comment: Pure BYOK; Aider itself has no vendor billing — users supply their own provider API key.

local-model-support.comment: Runs against local models via Ollama and any OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

multi-file-editing.comment: Plans and applies coordinated edits across multiple files; uses a repo map for whole-codebase context.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Can chain edit -> lint/test -> fix loops (auto-lint, auto-test, /test) and architect mode plans then applies; interactive but executes multi-step tasks with limited per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: /run and /test execute shell commands; auto-test/auto-lint can run build/test as part of the loop.

mcp-support.comment: Re-verified 2026-06-02: still no native MCP in core Aider; only third-party MCP servers/wrappers (e.g. disler/aider-mcp-server) bridge the gap. Open feature request remains.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Runs a single linear loop. Architect mode pairs two models (planner + editor) sequentially, not parallel/spawned subagents.

codebase-indexing.comment: Builds a 'repo map' from tree-sitter symbol extraction of files rather than a semantic embeddings index; ad-hoc map, not a vector store.

planning-mode.comment: Architect/editor mode plans the solution with one model then applies edits with another; /ask gives a read-only Q&A mode.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts images (add image files, /paste image from clipboard) for screenshot/design context.

browser-computer-use.comment: /web only scrapes a page to markdown; no ability to drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify changes.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Per RESEARCH.md guideline, false because subagents-parallel-agents is false: aider runs a single linear loop with no subagent mechanism at all, so there is nothing for the model to spawn autonomously. Architect mode pairs a planner and editor model sequentially under user control, not model-driven fan-out. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational terminal edits with the user in the loop; runs multi-step edit/test loops with approval. No inline tab completion or detached async runs.

inline-completion.comment: CLI/chat tool; no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Runs interactively in a focused terminal session; no dispatch-and-walk-away cloud/background execution.

persistent-memory.comment: No cross-session learned memory. Persistent context comes from user-maintained convention files loaded each session, not stored agent memory.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: /undo reverts the last aider-made git commit, restoring the working tree before a change; backed by its automatic git commits.

git-integration.comment: Core strength: auto-commits each change with generated messages; /commit, /git, /diff, /undo. Deep native git awareness.

pr-creation.comment: Commits to local git but has no built-in pull-request creation; PRs are a manual follow-up via git/GitHub.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project convention files (e.g. CONVENTIONS.md) loaded via /read, --read, or the .aider.conf.yml 'read:' directive. Note: these are NOT auto-discovered — the user must point aider at them explicitly (see project-memory-files).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Configurable lint/test commands but no programmable event hooks or plugin API for extending agent behavior.

permission-model.comment: Prompts before running shell commands and applying suggested commands; flags like --yes-always and auto-commit settings make the posture configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Runs entirely on the user's machine and can use fully local models (Ollama/OpenAI-compatible), so it can operate without any external SaaS.

privacy-mode.comment: Aider itself has no model and does not train on code, but it offers no explicit zero-training guarantee — whether code trains a model depends on the user-chosen provider. Anonymized usage analytics are collected by default (opt-out).

enterprise-controls.comment: Open-source single-user CLI; no SSO/audit/RBAC/VPC governance offering.

pricing-model.comment: The tool is free and open-source; users pay only their chosen LLM provider for API usage (BYOK). No Aider subscription.

free-tier.comment: Tool is entirely free; only external model API costs apply (and free model tiers like OpenRouter/Gemini exist).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Aider charges nothing; cost is only the user's own provider API usage.

maturity.comment: Stable, widely used GA tool with frequent releases and ~45.7k stars; polished CLI though still rapidly evolving.

community-size.comment: ~45.7k GitHub stars (45,680) as of check date; per RESEARCH.md thresholds Aider is explicitly a 'large' community example.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: No first-party marketplace/plugin ecosystem; community wrappers exist (e.g. third-party MCP servers, AiderDesk) but there is no official plugin platform.

project-memory-files.comment: Aider does NOT auto-load any repo instruction file. Conventions (CONVENTIONS.md, and optionally CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) must be listed explicitly under a .aider.conf.yml 'read:' directive or via /read; a contributor confirms aider only warns if a listed file is missing. Third-party blogs claiming aider 'reads AGENTS.md natively' are contradicted by the official docs and the maintainers' own guidance, so the auto-loaded-file convention is 'none'.

memory-scopes.comment: aider loads .aider.conf.yml from the home directory (user/global), the git repo root (project), and the current directory (folder), in that order with later files taking priority. No enterprise/org-managed policy scope.

scoped-rules-files.comment: No conditional rule activation: convention files configured via 'read:' are always applied to context, with no path/glob, file-type, or agent-requested scoping mechanism.

auto-memory.comment: Aider never writes cross-session memory on its own; persistent context comes only from user-authored convention files. No automatic memory layer to disable.

skill-support.comment: Has a fixed set of built-in slash commands but no mechanism for user-defined, reusable on-demand skills/recipes; conventions are always-on instruction files, not skills.

skill-invocation.comment: Not applicable — aider has no reusable-skill mechanism (skill-support is false).

always-on-surface.comment: Not applicable — no skills mechanism, so nothing is held in context as a skill surface.

skill-distribution.comment: Not applicable — aider has no skill install/share mechanism (marketplace, registry, etc.).

sandboxing-technology.comment: Runs directly on the user's machine with full privileges; /run and /test execute shell commands in the user's own environment. No kernel sandbox, container, VM, or filesystem jail — safety rests on the confirmation prompt alone. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Each suggested shell command is gated individually with a (Y)es/(N)o/(D)on't-ask-again prompt (per-command). File edits are auto-applied and auto-committed while commands are prompted, so reads/writes are gated differently from command execution (read-write-distinction). No command-prefix patterns, per-path, or per-domain rules; --yes-always is a single global override, not fine-grained. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: No declarative allow/deny rule engine. Commands either prompt each time or are blanket-approved via --yes-always; there are no allow/deny rules to express precedence over. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: Configured via predefined flags/toggles (--yes-always, --suggest-shell-commands, --auto-commits, --auto-lint, --auto-test, --dry-run) rather than user-authored allow/deny rules or a programmable permission hook/callback. These are preset postures, not a declarative rule engine. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No default gating of secrets. Aider reads .env files for its own config (API keys), but ships no built-in deny pattern or refusal protecting a project's .env/key files from being read or edited; the user must add files to .aiderignore manually. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: The command prompt's '(D)on't ask again' choice suppresses further prompts only for the current session; it is not written to durable config and does not survive a restart. No saved per-command allowlist. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: --yes-always (env AIDER_YES_ALWAYS / yes-always in .aider.conf.yml, or yes=True when scripting) auto-approves every confirmation for hands-off runs. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: No outbound-network gating during a run. Commands run with the user's full network access; /web fetches arbitrary URLs. No default block, domain allowlist, or proxy mechanism documented. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box, aider applies and auto-commits file edits without per-edit approval, but prompts before running suggested shell commands ((Y)es/(N)o/(D)on't ask again). Checked 2026-06-12.

auto-accept-mode.comment: No quick in-session intermediate auto-accept toggle (nothing like Claude Code's Shift+Tab). Aider already auto-applies edits by default, so there is no edit-accept mode to toggle; for shell commands the only non-prompting option is --yes-always, which is the full skip-all-confirmations bypass (captured under unsupervised-mode), not a lighter middle rung. --auto-accept-architect exists but is a command-line startup flag scoped to architect-mode edits, not an in-session mode switch. Checked 2026-07-18.
